metadata.description: NEM wholesale spot prices, ASX base + cap contract settlement prices, and forward curve data by state. Curated from AEMO QED, AER Wholesale Markets Quarterly, ASX Energy, and Leading Edge Energy monthly reviews.

sources.name: ASX Energy — Provisional Cash Settlement Prices
Leading Edge Energy — Monthly Market Reviews

metadata.notes: Cap contract prices are the $300 cap — the standard traded instrument. Cap price represents the average payout per MWh for intervals above $300/MWh during the quarter. Higher cap prices = more high-price events = better for BESS. Forward curve is ASX calendar-year base futures.

quarterly_spot_prices.description: Volume-weighted average spot prices by NEM region ($/MWh). Source: AEMO QED + AER.

data.note: June 2025 extreme prices — 3 days drove 85% of volatility
31% of intervals negative; SA 48.4% negative, VIC 43.1%

monthly_spot_prices.description: Monthly average spot prices for key months ($/MWh). Source: ASX Energy PCSP + Leading Edge Energy.

data.note: VIC lowest mainland price; NEM avg -37.8% YoY

cap_contract_prices.description: $300 cap contract quarterly settlement prices ($/MWh). Pays out average excess above $300/MWh per interval. Higher = more high-price events = better BESS economics. Source: ASX Energy PCSP.

data.note: VIC cap near zero — effectively no high-price events

cap_contract_prices.interpretation: Cap prices are the direct measure of BESS arbitrage opportunity. Declining cap prices = fewer/smaller price spikes = worse BESS revenue. QLD cap prices volatile but compressing as the battery fleet absorbs the spikes that used to drive cap value. SA cap spiked to $76.70 in Q3 2024 during Heywood outage — demonstrating how interconnector constraints create localised BESS opportunity.
cap_contract_prices.bess_revenue_impact: Modo estimates cap contracts could add $9.2k-$31.6k/MW/yr to BESS revenue. QLD shows the largest potential gain (10-15% of merchant earnings). But shorter-duration batteries (<4hr) can only defend 20-40% of nameplate capacity — limiting actual cap revenue realisation.

forward_curve.description: ASX calendar-year base futures by state ($/MWh). Represents market's expectation of average wholesale price.

may_2026_close.note: QLD forwards fell ~$6-7/MWh since Dec 2025. Now flat at ~$79/MWh through CY29.

curve_shape_analysis.nsw: Flat at ~$104/MWh. Eraring retirement not yet fully priced — if closure accelerates, forward curve should steepen. Asymmetric upside for BESS.
curve_shape_analysis.qld: Flat at ~$79/MWh. No recovery priced. Market expects permanently suppressed volatility. Worst forward curve for BESS investment.
curve_shape_analysis.vic: Backwardated then rising: $75 CY26 → $83 CY29. Yallourn closure (mid 2028) being gradually priced in. VIC BESS case improves over time.
curve_shape_analysis.sa: Gently rising: $88 CY26 → $94 CY29. Reflects continued interconnection constraints + industrial demand growth (hydrogen, minerals). Most BESS-friendly curve.

spread_analysis.description: Peak-to-trough spread is the key metric for BESS economics. Wider spread = more arbitrage revenue.

nsw.outlook: Moderate; Eraring retirement should widen significantly

qld.outlook: Lowest in NEM; was $244/MWh — most compressed

vic.outlook: Low; Yallourn closure should widen from 2028

sa.outlook: Highest by far — 4x QLD — interconnection constraints

spread_analysis.investment_signal: Watch the QLD forward peak-to-trough spread on ASX. When it starts widening, the market is pricing in coal retirement scarcity. That is the signal to re-enter QLD BESS. Current signal: flat/compressing.

rationale.sa: Highest spreads ($328/MWh), rising forward curve, interconnection scarcity creates localised BESS opportunity. Limited by connection availability.
rationale.nsw: Eraring 2.88 GW retirement (2027-29) is the single largest supply removal in NEM history. Forward curve doesn't fully price it. Asymmetric BESS opportunity — but timing risk if closure is delayed.
rationale.vic: Yallourn 1.48 GW retirement (mid 2028) will tighten supply. Forward curve gradually pricing it in ($75→$83). BESS case improves with patience.
rationale.qld: Flat forward curve, smallest coal retirement (Callide B 700 MW), most saturated BESS fleet, worst spreads. Last priority for new merchant BESS capital.
